scope_note: QA Witt Tower Geomagnetic Storm Dst Orbit Discriminator cert [452]. Seventh domain in Witt tower empirical chain: geomagnetism / space weather. Feature: mean hourly Dst index (nT) per window. Data: NASA OMNI2 hourly Dst, August-September 2017 (1464 hourly windows); doi:10.48322/45bb-8792 (King & Papitashvili 2005). Event class: storm main phase = 17 hourly windows with Dst < -100 nT from September 8-9 2017 G4 geomagnetic storm (coincident with X9.3 solar flare and cert [449] SEP event). Background: 1447 quiet/moderate hours. QA mapping: scalar Dst (nT) per window rank-normalized among all 1464 windows (ascending, most negative = rank 0); bin = floor(rank * 27 / 1464) in Z/27Z; T0=bins 0-8 (Singularity), T1=9-17 (Satellite), T2=18-26 (Cosmos). Theorem NT: Dst values (nT, float) are observer projections; rank bin in Z/27Z is QA integer state. CERTIFIED FACTS: (C1) n_storm=17, n_quiet=1447, n_total=1464 PASS. (C2) storm_mean=-117.4 nT < -100 AND quiet_mean=-19.4 nT > -30 PASS. (C3) ALL 17 storm hours in T0; K_t0=488, log10_p=-8.19 < -7.0 PASS. (C4) |storm_mean - quiet_mean| = 98.0 nT > 90.0 PASS. (C5) storm tiers={T0}; quiet spans {T0,T1,T2} PASS. (C6) peak_storm_dst=-148 nT < -120 (G3+ minimum) PASS. Orbital polarity symmetry: cert [449] SEP max-flux -> T2 (Cosmos); cert [452] storm min-Dst -> T0 (Singularity); antipodal in QA orbit space. PRIMARY SOURCES: King & Papitashvili (2005) doi:10.48322/45bb-8792 (OMNI2 dataset); Sugiura (1964) doi:10.1029/GM009p0001 (Dst index definition). Structural parent: cert [110]. Empirical chain: certs [442]-[451]. Seventh domain: geomagnetism. Validated 2026-06-18.
